lede: A look at January 21 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Luke Littler and Oskars Bārtulis, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2025 Kartalkaya hotel fire
events.desc: A fire at the Grand Kartal Hotel in the Kartalkaya ski resort in Bolu Province, Turkey, results in 78 people dead and 51 injured.

events.title: 2023 Monterey Park shooting
events.desc: Huu Can Tran, 72, opens fire in a dance studio in Monterey Park, California, killing eleven people and injuring nine others before later committing suicide. It is the worst mass shooting in Los Angeles County since the 2008 Covina massacre.

events.title: 2017 Women's March
events.desc: Over 400 cities across America and 160+ countries worldwide participate in a large-scale women's march, including in Portland, on Donald Trump's first full day as President of the United States.

events.title: 2011 Albanian opposition demonstrations
events.desc: Anti-government demonstrations take place in Tirana, Albania. Four people die from gunshots, allegedly fired from armed police protecting the Prime Minister's office.

events.title: United States House of Representatives
events.desc: The U.S. House of Representatives votes 395–28 to reprimand Newt Gingrich for ethics violations, making him the first Speaker of the House to be so disciplined.

events.title: Concorde
events.desc: Commercial service of Concorde begins with the London-Bahrain and Paris-Rio routes.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War, Battle of Khe Sanh: One of the most publicized and controversial battles of the war begins.

events.title: Boeing B-52 Stratofortress
events.desc: A B-52 bomber crashes near Thule Air Base, contaminating the area after its nuclear payload ruptures. One of the four bombs remains unaccounted for after the cleanup operation is complete.

events.title: Isaac Isaacs
events.desc: Sir Isaac Isaacs is sworn in as the first Australian-born Governor-General of Australia.

events.title: 1st Dáil
events.desc: A revolutionary Irish parliament is founded and declares the independence of the Irish Republic. One of the first engagements of the Irish War of Independence takes place.

births.name: Doug Weight
births.line: American ice hockey player and coach

deaths.text: Chumpol Silpa-archa, Thai academic and politician, Deputy Prime Minister of Thailand
Charlie Gehringer, American baseball player and manager
Carl "Alfalfa" Switzer, American child actor and hunting guide
Johann Georg Gichtel, German mystic and critic

observances.text: Christian feast day: John Yi Yun-il (one of The Korean Martyrs)
